sanskrit:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः।
मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः॥
transliteration:
ahaṅkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ ca saṃśritāḥ |
mām ātma-para-deheṣu pradviṣanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having taken shelter of false-ego, strength, pride, lust and anger, they blaspheme Me in their own and others’ bodies, being envious.
contextual_meaning: The demonic personality is colonized by five grandiose affects—ego-inflation, brute force, hubris, addictive craving, and rage—that fuse into a paranoid envy toward any reminder of a higher Self within oneself or anyone else.
psychological_state: Narcissistic defense against existential vulnerability: the person externalizes an intolerable inner God-image, then attacks it in self and others to preserve a brittle superiority.
trigger_scenario: A corporate climber learns a colleague meditates and quietly helps others; he mocks them as ‘weak’ and sabotages their project to prove ‘only results matter.’
anchor_text: Envy of the divine in others only imprisons the envier in his own false self.
